title: Scotty3D — Computer Graphics Application
summary: Scotty3D is a software 3D graphics application. This task requires implementing scene-graph transform composition (Assignment 1) and halfedge mesh editing operations (Assignment 2): local operations that rewire individual edges and faces, and global operations that reprocess the entire mesh topology. Three files are patched: src/scene/transform.cpp, src/geometry/halfedge-local.cpp, and src/geometry/halfedge-global.cpp.
instructionPreview: This is the Scotty3D 3D graphics teaching codebase. The project implements a software rasterizer, path tracer, and half-edge mesh editor. Multiple core modules have been left unimplemented (returning stubs) and must be filled in so that all tests pass.
instruction:
This is the Scotty3D 3D graphics teaching codebase. The project implements a software rasterizer, path tracer, and half-edge mesh editor. Multiple core modules have been left unimplemented (returning stubs) and must be filled in so that all tests pass.

Your working directory is `/workspace`. The project code is already present. Build with `node Maekfile.js -j4` from `/workspace`.

You need to implement all the modules and features listed below so that all tests pass.

## Modules to Implement

The following modules need to be implemented or substantially built out:

- **Scene-graph transforms** (`src/scene/transform.cpp`): Implements the local-to-world and world-to-local matrix computations by walking the parent chain of the scene graph. These must compose the transforms without calling `Mat4::inverse()`. (~2 functions)

- **Halfedge local operations** (`src/geometry/halfedge-local.cpp`): Core half-edge mesh editing operations including edge bisection, edge splitting with face subdivision, edge flipping for general polygon faces, edge collapse with manifold validity checking, and face extrusion with position updates. Each operation must correctly maintain the half-edge data structure invariants, handle boundary cases, and propagate interpolated vertex/corner data. (~6 functions)

- **Halfedge global operations** (`src/geometry/halfedge-global.cpp`): The Catmull-Clark subdivision helper that performs the actual topological split (splitting every edge, splitting every non-boundary face into quads around a central vertex, and updating vertex positions from provided maps). Also includes corner normal computation with sharp-edge smoothing groups, face orientation flipping, and per-face UV coordinate assignment. (~4 functions)

## Test-Coupled Signatures

The following functions are called directly by the test suite. You must implement them with these exact signatures:

- `Mat4 Transform::local_to_world() const` -- compute the local-to-world transformation matrix by composing transforms up the parent chain
- `Mat4 Transform::world_to_local() const` -- compute the world-to-local transformation matrix by composing inverse transforms up the parent chain
- `std::optional<Halfedge_Mesh::VertexRef> Halfedge_Mesh::bisect_edge(EdgeRef e)` -- split an edge without splitting adjacent faces, placing a new vertex at the midpoint
- `std::optional<Halfedge_Mesh::VertexRef> Halfedge_Mesh::split_edge(EdgeRef e)` -- split an edge and adjacent non-boundary faces by inserting a midpoint vertex and connecting to CCW vertices
- `std::optional<Halfedge_Mesh::EdgeRef> Halfedge_Mesh::flip_edge(EdgeRef e)` -- rotate a non-boundary edge CCW within its containing faces, working for general polygons
- `std::optional<Halfedge_Mesh::VertexRef> Halfedge_Mesh::collapse_edge(EdgeRef e)` -- merge edge endpoints into a midpoint vertex, handling triangle and polygon faces, rejecting non-manifold results
- `std::optional<Halfedge_Mesh::FaceRef> Halfedge_Mesh::extrude_face(FaceRef f)` -- create an inset face with surrounding quad side faces
- `void Halfedge_Mesh::extrude_positions(FaceRef face, Vec3 move, float shrink)` -- update vertex positions for an extruded face by shrinking toward centroid and translating
- `void Halfedge_Mesh::catmark_subdivide_helper(...)` -- perform the topological Catmull-Clark subdivision: split every edge, split every non-boundary face into quads around a central vertex, and update vertex positions
- `void Halfedge_Mesh::flip_orientation()` -- reverse the direction of all halfedge loops, swapping face winding order
- `void Halfedge_Mesh::set_corner_normals(float threshold)` -- compute face-corner normals using smoothing groups defined by sharp edges and angle threshold
- `void Halfedge_Mesh::set_corner_uvs_per_face()` -- assign UV coordinates per-face by projecting onto a plane perpendicular to each face normal

Use `interpolate_data({src1, src2}, dst)` to propagate halfedge corner data (UV, normals) and vertex bone weights when creating new mesh elements.

moduleDag.description: Scotty3D is a software 3D graphics application. This task requires implementing scene-graph transform composition (Assignment 1) and halfedge mesh editing operations (Assignment 2): local operations that rewire individual edges and faces, and global operations that reprocess the entire mesh topology. Three files are patched: src/scene/transform.cpp, src/geometry/halfedge-local.cpp, and src/geometry/halfedge-global.cpp.

nodes.label: Scene-Graph Transform Composition
nodes.description: Compute local_to_world() and world_to_local() by recursively composing TRS transforms up the scene-graph parent chain. Must not call Mat4::inverse(); instead uses the pre-existing parent_to_local() helper for inversion.

nodes.label: Halfedge Edge Bisection (bisect_edge)
nodes.description: Split an edge at its midpoint into two sub-edges without touching adjacent faces. Allocates one new vertex (vm), two new halfedges, and one new edge; rewires all twin/next/vertex/edge/face pointers; propagates interpolated corner and bone-weight data. Foundational building block called directly by split_edge.

nodes.label: Halfedge Edge Flip (flip_edge)
nodes.description: Rotate a non-boundary edge CCW within its two containing faces, generalising to arbitrary polygon faces. Rewires halfedge connectivity without allocating new mesh elements, then validates the result is manifold.

nodes.label: Halfedge Edge Collapse (collapse_edge)
nodes.description: Merge the two endpoints of an edge into a single midpoint vertex, removing the edge and adjacent faces that would degenerate. Must detect and reject results that would create a non-manifold mesh and propagate interpolated vertex data.

nodes.label: Halfedge Face Extrusion (extrude_face + extrude_positions)
nodes.description: extrude_face creates an inset copy of a face surrounded by new quad side-faces, allocating new vertices, halfedges, edges, and faces. extrude_positions then updates those vertex positions by shrinking the inset face toward its centroid and translating it along the face normal by a given distance.

nodes.label: Halfedge Edge Split (split_edge)
nodes.description: Split an edge and its adjacent non-boundary triangle faces by inserting a midpoint vertex and connecting it to the opposite vertex of each adjacent face, creating two new faces per side. Calls bisect_edge to perform the initial edge bisection, then adds the connecting diagonal half-edges into each adjacent face.

nodes.label: Catmull-Clark Topological Subdivision Helper (catmark_subdivide_helper)
nodes.description: Perform the full topological Catmull-Clark subdivision of the entire mesh: split every edge (same concept as split_edge applied to all edges), split every non-boundary face into quads around a newly-inserted face-center vertex, then update all vertex positions from pre-computed position maps. Called by catmark_subdivide() after it computes the new positions.

nodes.label: Global Mesh Orientation, Normals, and UV Utilities
nodes.description: Three independent global mesh utilities: flip_orientation reverses all halfedge loop winding to flip face normals; set_corner_normals computes per-corner normals using smoothing groups defined by sharp edges and an angle threshold; set_corner_uvs_per_face assigns UV coordinates by projecting each face vertex onto a tangent plane perpendicular to the face normal.

edges.label: split_edge calls bisect_edge directly to perform the initial edge midpoint insertion before adding the diagonal connecting half-edges into each adjacent face

catmark_subdivide_helper performs the conceptually identical operation (split every mesh edge, rewire face loops into quads around a center vertex) that split_edge does for a single edge; the course sequences local ops (A2L checkpoint) before global ops (A2G checkpoint) because understanding individual halfedge rewiring is prerequisite for implementing the all-at-once version
